meta.phase-name: Dolt Workflow Contract and Governance Baseline

phase-plan.purpose: Define the end-to-end Dolt authoring lineage workflow (who uses it, how patches are promoted, and how it interoperates with Supabase runtime distribution) without coupling runtime delivery to Dolt.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: role definitions, branch/merge/promotion model, parity checks against JSON patch ingest, release handoff contract. Out of scope: plugin runtime dependency on Dolt and immediate production cutover.

file.description: Current optional Dolt ingest source used for build-time patch merge.
Published E2E workflow baseline and role mapping.

task.description: Define branch strategy and merge gates for Dolt-backed content patch authoring.
Define artifact promotion rule from Dolt patch state to signed runtime release artifacts in Supabase.
Define role-specific runbooks for content designers, release engineers, and platform ops.

questions.question: What Dolt branch model best matches team ownership (main/release/feature vs environment branches)?
What exact approvals are required before promoting Dolt-derived patches to downloadable runtime artifacts?

decision.summary: Phase 53 is intentionally planned, not executed yet, so phase 52 can ship runtime delivery first.
decision.impact: Prevents scope creep and keeps current release priorities clear.

suggestion.gap: No formal requirement yet defines Dolt branch governance and promotion controls for content patches.
suggestion.proposal: Add explicit governance requirement for Dolt patch lifecycle and approval policy before runtime publish.
